1	# 03 — Engineering Specifications
2	
3	**Purpose:** All functional/non-functional requirements, API contracts, data models, acceptance criteria.  
4	**Scope:** Phase 2 implementation requirements — what must be built and how it will be verified.  
5	**Source:** `Cachelane_Phase2_Engineering_Specifications_v2_1.docx` (v2, May 2026).
6	
7	> **Section-numbering note:** The original doc had a printing bug where §6 "Agent implementation
8	> guardrails" printed its subsections as §7.1–7.7. Fixed in v2 — all references below use the
9	> corrected §6.x numbering.
10	
11	---
12	
13	## Functional Requirements
14	
15	### REQ-F-001 through REQ-F-010 — Transport & Storage Foundation
16	
17	| ID | Requirement | Source |
18	|----|-------------|--------|
19	| REQ-F-001 | The system SHALL be distributed as an MCP server using `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (TypeScript) over **stdio transport** (no network ports). | §1.1, D8 |
20	| REQ-F-002 | The orchestrator SHALL read Anthropic API usage fields on every turn: `cache_creation_input_tokens`, `cache_read_input_tokens`, `ephemeral_5m_input_tokens`, `ephemeral_1h_input_tokens` via `@anthropic-ai/sdk`. | §1.1 |
21	| REQ-F-003 | Block-size accounting SHALL use `@anthropic-ai/tokenizer` with a **model-string lookup** to select the correct tokenizer per request (supports both Opus 4.6 and 4.7). | §1.1, Q8 |
22	| REQ-F-004 | Persistent storage SHALL be a single-file SQLite database via `better-sqlite3` in WAL mode. No external store. | §1.1, §1.2.2, D5 |
23	| REQ-F-005 | In-memory cache-state tracking SHALL live in the orchestrator process using plain `Map`/`Set`; restart recovery relies on the on-disk SQLite log as the authoritative source. | §1.1 |
24	| REQ-F-006 | Hashing for prefix fingerprinting and block deduplication SHALL use SHA-256 via `node:crypto`. | §1.1 |
25	| REQ-F-007 | The CLI SHALL be implemented with `commander`. | §1.1 |
26	| REQ-F-008 | Logging SHALL be structured JSON via `pino`, with `pino-pretty` in dev. Log lines SHALL include billable usage fields. | §1.1, §1.4 |
27	| REQ-F-009 | Local logs SHALL be written to `~/.cachelane/logs/`, **rotated daily**, with **7-day retention**. | §1.4 |
28	| REQ-F-010 | The system SHALL expose a `cachelane stats` CLI command that reads directly from the SQLite log (no external metrics service). | §1.4 |
29	
30	### REQ-F-011 through REQ-F-020 — Telemetry, Privacy & Security
31	
32	| ID | Requirement | Source |
33	|----|-------------|--------|
34	| REQ-F-011 | The system SHALL implement opt-in anonymous telemetry reporting cache-hit ratio, average reduction, and model used; **default OFF**; permanently disable-able. | §1.4 |
35	| REQ-F-012 | Telemetry MUST NOT be enabled by default; activation requires an explicit command. | §1.4, Q7, D9 |
36	| REQ-F-013 | All processing SHALL occur locally. No request bodies, prompts, or completions may leave the user's machine except in the direct path to `api.anthropic.com`. | §1.5, D7 |
37	| REQ-F-014 | The reference log SHALL store **block IDs, classifications, token counts, usage counters only** — NOT block contents. | §1.5 |
38	| REQ-F-015 | Block contents SHALL live only transiently in the orchestrator process and SHALL NOT be persisted. | §1.5 |
39	| REQ-F-016 | The Anthropic API key SHALL be read from the user's existing Claude Code config; Cachelane MUST NOT read, store, or forward it outside the originating API call. | §1.5 |
40	| REQ-F-017 | The system SHALL respect Anthropic workspace-level cache isolation (effective since Feb 2026): Cachelane MUST use Claude Code's existing workspace ID and MUST NOT share prefixes across workspaces. | §1.5, Q6 |
41	| REQ-F-018 | The reference log SHALL be keyed by workspace ID. | Q6 |
42	| REQ-F-019 | The system SHALL implement a synthetic **keepalive ping** mechanism: a minimal API call (`max_tokens=1`, one-token user message) that touches the same prefix to reset its TTL. | §2.1 |
43	| REQ-F-020 | Keepalive pings MUST NOT fire when a real turn just touched the cache (idle-only triggering). | §2.2, B1 |
44	
45	### REQ-F-021 through REQ-F-030 — Orchestration Logic
46	
47	| ID | Requirement | Source |
48	|----|-------------|--------|
49	| REQ-F-021 | The default ship configuration SHALL be a **hybrid keepalive policy**: adaptive 4-minute idle trigger for short prefixes, automatically switching to 1-hour TTL when prefix size P > 50k tokens. | §2.4.6 |
50	| REQ-F-022 | A `cachelane.config.json` (`~/.cachelane/config.json`) SHALL expose manual override knobs for keepalive policy and all other configurable parameters. | §2.4.6 |
51	| REQ-F-023 | The K-pruner SHALL use a **three-signal deterministic reference detector**: (a) file paths quoted in tool calls, (b) block IDs cited in assistant text, (c) 40-character shingle exact substring overlap. | §1.2.3, Q1 |
52	| REQ-F-024 | Cachelane SHALL inject a short ID prefix on every stubbable block (used by the reference detector and for refetch via `cachelane:expand`). | §1.2.3 |
53	| REQ-F-025 | The system MUST NOT use embeddings, vector stores, or any ML model dependency for reference detection. | §1.2.3, D6 |
54	| REQ-F-026 | The K-pruner default value of K SHALL be **K=3**, with `--aggressive` mode (K=2) and `--conservative` mode (K=5). | Q3 |
55	| REQ-F-027 | The classifier SHALL be fingerprint-based and **conservative**: a block defaults to `VOLATILE` unless it matches a stable signature (hash of file paths + last-modified mtimes for stable candidates). | Q2 |
56	| REQ-F-028 | The reorderer MUST NOT reorder `tool_use`/`tool_result` pairs individually; it MAY only move whole pairs as a unit (invariant preserves byte-identical prefix). | Q9 |
57	| REQ-F-029 | A second `cache_control` breakpoint at the middle-suffix boundary SHALL be placed **only if** the same turn-window has been seen byte-identical at least twice (dynamic placement). | Q4 |
58	| REQ-F-030 | When Claude Code's `/compact` runs, Cachelane SHALL treat the new compacted history as a fresh middle region and reset all per-block counters for replaced blocks. | Q5 |
59	
60	### REQ-F-031 through REQ-F-037 — Distribution & Packaging
61	
62	| ID | Requirement | Source |
63	|----|-------------|--------|
64	| REQ-F-031 | The interaction with `/compact` SHALL be documented in the README. | Q5 |
65	| REQ-F-032 | The system SHALL be packaged as an npm package and dual-published as a Claude Code plugin via `.claude-plugin` manifest. | §1.1 |
66	| REQ-F-033 | Releases SHALL be signed via **npm provenance** through GitHub Actions. | §1.1 |
67	| REQ-F-034 | Build/bundling SHALL be performed with `tsup` (esbuild), emitting both ESM and CJS. | §1.1 |
68	| REQ-F-035 | The CLI SHALL provide a privacy doc in the README and a `cachelane stats --opt-in` command for telemetry opt-in. | Q7, D9 |
69	| REQ-F-036 | `[INFERRED]` Stubs created by the K-pruner SHALL preserve the block's identifier and be refetchable on demand — pruning is non-lossy at the application layer. | Project context |
70	| REQ-F-037 | `[INFERRED]` The orchestrator SHALL forward requests directly to `api.anthropic.com` on the user's behalf (no gateway). | §3.3 |
71

102	### REQ-NF-017 through REQ-NF-029 — Cost & Metrics
103	
104	| ID | Requirement | Target | Source |
105	|----|-------------|--------|--------|
106	| REQ-NF-017 | Total agent token spend target | **$180–$340** to v1.0 | §3.4.1 |
107	| REQ-NF-018 | Upfront cash spend | **$0** (or $20 optional domain; $80 if separate API account) | §3.2 |
108	| REQ-NF-019 | Recurring cash spend | **$0** (up to $10/mo only if telemetry enabled and > 100k users) | §3.3 |

110	| REQ-NF-021 | Cache-stability test (breakpoint-change PRs) | Must pass **twice on independent runs** | §6.5 |
111	| REQ-NF-022 | Effective input-token cost formula | `input_tokens + 1.25×cache_creation_5m + 2.0×cache_creation_1h + 0.1×cache_read` | §2.4.4 |
112	| REQ-NF-023 | Cache hit ratio formula | `cache_read_input_tokens / (input + cache_creation + cache_read)` | §2.4.4 |
113	| REQ-NF-024 | Latency metric | Wall-clock time-to-first-token on first real turn after a pause | §2.4.4 |

119	
120	---
121	
122	## API Contracts
123	
124	### C-1: Anthropic Messages API (south-bound, consumed)
125	
126	- **Client SDK:** `@anthropic-ai/sdk`
127	- **Required response fields read per turn:**
128	  - `cache_creation_input_tokens`
129	  - `cache_read_input_tokens`
130	  - `ephemeral_5m_input_tokens`
131	  - `ephemeral_1h_input_tokens`
132	- **Cache semantics:** byte-exact prefix matching with TTL. Workspace-isolated since Feb 2026.
133	- **Pricing:** 0.1× read, 1.25× 5-min write, 2.0× 1-hour write.
134	
135	### C-2: Keepalive Ping Request
136	
137	- **Payload:** one-token user message; `max_tokens=1`; identical prefix to current session
138	- **Effect:** resets the prefix's TTL
139	- **Per-ping billable cost (model):** ~`0.1 × P + 5` base-input-token units
140	
141	### C-3: MCP Server (Cachelane ↔ Claude Code)
142	
143	- **Transport:** stdio (no network port)
144	- **SDK:** `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` ^1.x
145	- **Host requirement:** Claude Code ≥ 0.6
146	- **MCP tools exposed:** `cachelane:stats`, `cachelane:explain`, `cachelane:expand`
147	
148	### C-4: Telemetry Endpoint (opt-in, operated by project)
149	
150	- **URL:** `https://telemetry.cachelane.dev/v1/report`
151	- **Default state:** OFF
152	- **Payload fields (allowed):** `installation_id` (random UUID), `version`, `reporting_period_ms`, `turns`, `cache_hit_ratio`, `pruner_enabled`, `keepalive_policy`, `effective_cost_units_total`, `no_cachelane_baseline_total`
153	- **Payload fields (forbidden):** `workspace_id`, `session_id`, file paths, block contents, model name, API key, IP-correlatable timestamps
154	- **Rate:** at most once per hour per installation
155	- **Response:** 204
156	
157	### C-5: CLI Surface (commander)
158	
159	```
160	cachelane install
161	cachelane stats [--since <ISO-duration>]
162	cachelane stats --opt-in
163	cachelane explain [--turn <N>]
164	cachelane pin <file|glob>
165	cachelane exclude <file|glob>
166	cachelane prune --aggressive | --conservative | --default
167	cachelane keepalive <off|static|adaptive|auto>
168	cachelane disable
169	cachelane enable
170	cachelane doctor
171	cachelane uninstall [--purge]
172	```
173	
174	### C-6: Configuration File
175	
176	- **Path:** `~/.cachelane/config.json`
177	- **Schema version field:** `"version": 1`
178	- **Key fields (with defaults):**
179	
180	| Field | Default | Range/values | Effect |
181	|-------|---------|-------------|--------|
182	| `pruner.enabled` | `true` | boolean | Toggle K-pruning |
183	| `pruner.k` | `3` | 1–10 | Idle-turn threshold |
184	| `pruner.mode` | `"default"` | `default \| conservative \| aggressive` | Overrides K |
185	| `keepalive.policy` | `"auto"` | `off \| static \| adaptive \| auto` | Keepalive strategy |
186	| `keepalive.interval_seconds` | `150` | positive int | Check loop interval |
187	| `keepalive.idle_threshold_seconds` | `240` | positive int | Min idle before ping |
188	| `keepalive.large_prefix_threshold_tokens` | `50000` | positive int | Switch to 1h TTL above this |
189	| `classification.sliding_window_turns` | `4` | positive int | Recent-turn window size |
190	| `telemetry.opt_in` | `false` | boolean | Telemetry opt-in |
191	| `log_level` | `"info"` | `trace \| debug \| info \| warn \| error` | Pino log level |
192	
193	- **Migration:** newer-than-supported config schema → refuse to start; older → run migration
194	
195	---
196	
197	## Data Models
198	
199	> **Convention:** storage and API-contract types use `snake_case` (e.g. the `Block` interface
200	> below, the `blocks/turns/block_references` rows, and `CachelaneConfig` fields). In-process
201	> working types (function parameters, request payloads handled in code) may use `camelCase`.
202	> Rule of thumb: if it crosses a process / storage / network boundary, snake_case.
203	
204	### Entity: Block (in-memory, transient)
205	
206	```typescript
207	interface Block {
208	  // Identity
209	  id: string;                       // ULID; assigned on first observation
210	  workspace_id: string;             // Claude Code workspace identifier
211	  session_id: string;               // Claude Code session identifier
212	
213	  // Classification
214	  kind: BlockKind;                  // see enum below — 11 values
215	  volatility: Volatility;           // "STABLE" | "SEMI" | "VOLATILE"
216	  is_pinned: boolean;               // true if matched by config.classification.pin
217	
218	  // Content (transient; never persisted)
219	  // content: AnthropicContentBlock — deferred to M2 (no consumer in M1)
220	  content_hash: string;             // SHA-256 of canonical serialization
221	
222	  // Accounting
223	  token_count: number;              // tokens under the current model's tokenizer
224	  added_at_turn: number;            // turn number when first observed
225	  last_referenced_at_turn: number;  // turn number of most recent reference
226	  unused_turns: number;             // counter for K-pruning; starts at 0
227	
228	  // Stub state
229	  is_stub: boolean;                 // true if currently materialized as a stub
230	  stub_summary: string | null;      // one-line summary if stubbed; else null
231	  refetch_handle: string | null;    // tool invocation to restore content
232	}
233	
234	type BlockKind =
235	  | "system_prompt"
236	  | "tool_schema"
237	  | "claude_md"
238	  | "project_rules"
239	  | "prior_turn"
240	  | "tool_use_result_pair"
241	  | "file_read"
242	  | "retrieval_result"
243	  | "tool_output"
244	  | "user_message"
245	  | "stub";
246	```
247	
248	### Entity: ReferenceLog row (SQLite `blocks` table)
249	
250	```sql
251	CREATE TABLE blocks (
252	  id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,         -- ULID
253	  workspace_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
254	  session_id      TEXT NOT NULL,
255	  content_hash    TEXT NOT NULL,
256	  kind            TEXT NOT NULL,
257	  volatility      TEXT NOT NULL,            -- "STABLE" | "SEMI" | "VOLATILE"
258	  is_pinned       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
259	  token_count     INTEGER NOT NULL,
260	  added_at_turn   INTEGER NOT NULL,
261	  last_referenced_at_turn INTEGER NOT NULL,
262	  unused_turns    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
263	  is_stub         INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
264	  stub_summary    TEXT,
265	  refetch_handle  TEXT,
266	  created_at      INTEGER NOT NULL,
267	  updated_at      INTEGER NOT NULL
268	);
269	CREATE INDEX idx_blocks_session ON blocks(workspace_id, session_id);
270	CREATE INDEX idx_blocks_hash    ON blocks(content_hash);
271	CREATE INDEX idx_blocks_unused  ON blocks(unused_turns) WHERE is_stub = 0;
272	```
273	
274	**Block contents are NEVER persisted** — only metadata.
275	
276	### Entity: Turn (SQLite `turns` table)
277	
278	```sql
279	CREATE TABLE turns (
280	  id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
281	  workspace_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
282	  session_id      TEXT NOT NULL,
283	  turn_number     INTEGER NOT NULL,
284	  model           TEXT NOT NULL,
285	  input_tokens    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
286	  output_tokens   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
287	  cache_creation_5m_tokens   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
288	  cache_creation_1h_tokens   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
289	  cache_read_tokens          INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
290	  effective_cost_units       REAL NOT NULL,  -- computed at write time
291	  prefix_breakpoint_hash     TEXT,
292	  middle_breakpoint_hash     TEXT,
293	  pruned_blocks_count        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
294	  keepalive_pings_since_last_turn INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
295	  created_at      INTEGER NOT NULL
296	);
297	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_turns_session_num ON turns(workspace_id, session_id, turn_number);
298	```
299	
300	Column names (`cache_creation_5m_tokens`, `cache_creation_1h_tokens`) deliberately mirror the
301	Anthropic API response fields (`ephemeral_5m_input_tokens`, `ephemeral_1h_input_tokens`,
302	`cache_creation_input_tokens`) so the orchestrator does not need a translation layer.
303	
304	### Entity: BlockReference (SQLite `block_references` table, append-only audit log)
305	
306	```sql
307	CREATE TABLE block_references (
308	  id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
309	  block_id        TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES blocks(id),
310	  turn_id         TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES turns(id),
311	  reference_type  TEXT NOT NULL,    -- "tool_call" | "text_quote" | "id_mention"
312	  evidence        TEXT NOT NULL,    -- short string showing what matched
313	  created_at      INTEGER NOT NULL
314	);
315	CREATE INDEX idx_refs_block ON block_references(block_id);
316	CREATE INDEX idx_refs_turn  ON block_references(turn_id);
317	-- 90-day retention cap (enforced at write time once retention job lands; M4+)
318	```
319	
320	### Entity: CacheStateTracker (in-memory only)
321	
322	```typescript
323	interface PrefixState {
324	  workspace_id: string;
325	  prefix_hash: string;
326	  middle_hash: string | null;
327	  prefix_token_count: number;
328	  ttl_class: "5m" | "1h";
329	  cached_at_ms: number;
330	  last_read_at_ms: number;
331	  expected_expiry_ms: number;
332	}
333	// Map<workspace_id, PrefixState>
334	// Resets on process restart; next turn pays one cache-write penalty
335	```
336	
337	### Entity: Turn (logical model)
338	
339	- One user message + one assistant reply (tool calls inside the reply do not split the turn)
340	- Contains zero or more `(tool_use, tool_result)` pairs
341	- The Reorderer moves `(tool_use, tool_result)` pairs **only as atomic units** (splitting would
342	  invalidate the prefix from that point on)

355	| AC-6 | The 100-session annotated corpus exists in-repo **before** any pruner code is written | §6.3 Step 1 |

371	
372	---
373	
374	## Edge Cases & Failure Modes
375	
376	| # | Scenario | Required behaviour |
377	|---|----------|--------------------|
378	| E1 | Process restart | SQLite reference log is authoritative; in-memory cache state resets; next turn pays one cache-write penalty |
379	| E2 | `/compact` runs | Reset `unused_turns` for all replaced blocks; classify compacted summary as `SEMI`; defer middle breakpoint until seen byte-identical twice |
380	| E3 | `(tool_use, tool_result)` pair split by reorderer | **Must not happen.** Pairs are only moved as atomic units (REQ-F-028). A split invalidates the prefix from that point. |
381	| E4 | Tokenizer drift Opus 4.6 → 4.7 | Up to 35% more tokens for same text; must use model-string lookup (REQ-F-003) or cost predictions are wrong |
382	| E5 | Cache TTL expiry between turns (idle session) | Keepalive worker mitigates; TTL expiry is also tracked as an eviction-event metric |
383	| E6 | Multi-workspace prefix sharing | Violates Anthropic's Feb 2026 isolation; must key reference log by workspace ID (REQ-F-017, REQ-F-018) |
384	| E7 | Middle-region speculative cache write (unstable middle) | Wastes 1.25× write; mitigated by the "twice seen" heuristic (REQ-F-029) |
385	| E8 | SQLite write failure | Log; drop the per-block counter update for this turn; **continue** (fail-open) |
386	| E9 | SQLite corruption (detected on startup) | Rename to `cachelane.db.corrupt-{timestamp}`; create new empty DB; warn user |
387	| E10 | Hook exception | Log stack; return unmutated request; pass-through for one turn (fail-open) |
388	| E11 | Non-deterministic serialisation / timestamp leakage in prefix | Cache-stability test catches this; `Date.now`, `Math.random`, `Map` iteration order are flagged in PR checklist |
389	| E12 | Refetch unavailable (block's original source gone) | `[INFERRED]` Must handle gracefully; return error to model, do not crash |
390	| E13 | Two Claude Code instances sharing same SQLite | WAL mode handles concurrent readers + serialised writers safely |
391	| E14 | `better-sqlite3` native build failure | Prebuilt binaries used; `cachelane doctor` checks Node version |
392	| E15 | Config schema newer than supported | Refuse to start (avoid silent demotion) |
393	| E16 | Config schema older than current | Run migration automatically |
394

1	# 01 — System Overview
2	
3	**Purpose:** High-level product summary, goals, non-goals, and full glossary.  
4	**Scope:** Product-level understanding; no implementation details.  
5	**Sources:** All five source documents.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## What Cachelane Is
10	
11	Cachelane is a **local MCP server + Claude Code hooks** (PreRequest/PostResponse) that intercepts
12	every conversation turn between Claude Code and `api.anthropic.com`. It does two things:
13	
14	1. **Cache-aware prompt orchestration** — classifies each content block by volatility and reorders
15	   the prompt into three regions (stable prefix, semi-stable middle, volatile suffix), placing two
16	   `cache_control` breakpoints so Anthropic's prompt cache fires at **0.1×** the normal input cost
17	   on cache hits instead of paying **1.0×** every turn.
18	
19	2. **K-pruning** — after each turn, any tool-call result block that has been idle for ≥ K
20	   consecutive turns is replaced with a compact stub that preserves its identifier and is
21	   refetchable on demand. This flattens token growth in long sessions without discarding any
22	   information.
23	
24	Cachelane is **a complement to, not a replacement for**, existing context tools (Context Mode,
25	Code Graph, Token Savior, etc.). It operates on the final assembled prompt, after every other tool
26	has shaped its portion of context.
27	
28	**Positioning tag:** "A cache-discipline layer for Claude Code."
29	
30	---
31	
32	## Goals
33	
34	| # | Goal |
35	|---|------|
36	| G1 | Reduce input-token costs on every subsequent turn via K-pruning |
37	| G2 | Maximize Anthropic prompt-cache hit rate by reordering blocks into stable/semi/volatile regions with two `cache_control` breakpoints |
38	| G3 | Refresh prompt-cache TTL via a keepalive worker so users don't pay full re-write penalties after idle periods |
39	| G4 | Fail open: never make Claude Code slower or less reliable than without Cachelane |
40	| G5 | Provide local-only operation — zero-cost regime, per-user, no shared backend |
41	| G6 | Respect Anthropic workspace cache isolation (effective Feb 5, 2026) |
42	
43	---
44	
45	## Non-Goals
46	
47	| # | Non-Goal | Disposition |
48	|---|----------|-------------|
49	| NG1 | Tokenizer-aware content rewriting | Deferred to v1.1 (Phase 1 M3) |
50	| NG2 | Speculative / budgeted block inclusion | Deferred (overlaps Token Savior) |
51	| NG3 | Cross-session decision distillation | Rejected (overlaps memsearch) |
52	| NG4 | Shared team caches / multi-user fleet | Out of scope; would leave the zero-cost regime |
53	| NG5 | Hosted backend or cloud infrastructure | Explicitly rejected (see [ADR-007](decisions/ADR-007-local-only.md)) |
54	| NG6 | Retry of Anthropic API 5xx errors | That is the API client's responsibility |
55	| NG7 | Tracker reconstruction on process restart | Deferred to v1.1 (one cache-write penalty on cold start is acceptable) |
56	
57	---
58	
59	## Product Positioning
60	
61	**The gap Cachelane fills.** Seven major Claude Code token-reduction tools converged on four
62	reduction axes: upstream filtering, structural pre-indexing, progressive disclosure, and
63	output-side compression. None of them addresses the **prompt cache** — the largest single
64	unexploited cost lever in the Claude API.
65	
66	Reported real-world cache-hit ratios *after deliberate prefix design* are **50–84%**
67	(Helicone, Vellum, ProjectDiscovery). Rates below 20% are classified as a prefix-design failure,
68	not a model limitation. The naively-routed prompt pays 1.0× input cost every turn instead of 0.1×
69	on cache reads — a missed ~10× discount on the largest cost surface.
70	
71	**Expected impact (v1):**
72	- Cache-aware orchestration (M1): **30–60% savings** on cacheable workloads
73	- K-pruning (M2): additional **+10–15 percentage points** on 15-turn sessions at K=3
74	- Combined target: ≥ 40% effective input-cost reduction vs. the B1 starter stack baseline
75	
76	---
77	
78	## Anthropic Prompt Cache Mechanics
79	
80	| Operation | Price multiplier | Notes |
81	|-----------|-----------------|-------|
82	| Cache read | **0.1×** base input | 90% discount |
83	| 5-minute cache write | **1.25×** base input | Default TTL |
84	| 1-hour cache write | **2.0×** base input | Break-even at 2 reads |
85	| Cache match requirement | byte-identical prefix to `cache_control` breakpoint | Any change invalidates from that point |
86	| Workspace isolation | per-workspace | Enforced by Anthropic since Feb 5, 2026 |
87	
88	**Worked example:** 30k-token prefix reused across 10 turns.
89	- Naïve (no cache): 1.0× × 10 = **10.0 prefix-units**
90	- Cache-aware: 1.25× (one write) + 0.1× × 9 (nine reads) = **2.15 prefix-units** → **4.6× reduction**
91	
92	---
93	
94	## Glossary
95	
96	| Term | Definition |
97	|------|------------|
98	| **Turn** | One user message plus the assistant's full reply (tool calls inside the reply do not split the turn). |
99	| **Tool call** | An assistant-initiated function invocation. Multiple tool calls per turn are allowed; they do not advance the turn counter. |
100	| **Content block** | A single tool-call result; the atomic unit that K-pruning operates on. |

1	# 06 — Systems Design
2	
3	**Purpose:** Technology stack, module layout, data schemas, performance budgets, failure modes, and release milestones.  
4	**Scope:** Implementation-level reference — the "how" of building Cachelane.  
5	**Source:** `Cachelane_Systems_Design_Document_1.docx` (v1.0, May 2026).
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Goals & Non-Goals
10	
11	**Goals:**
12	- Reduce input-token costs via K-pruning (non-lossy, refetchable stubs)
13	- Maximise cache hits via block reordering + two `cache_control` breakpoints
14	- Refresh prompt cache TTL via keepalive worker
15	- **Fail open:** never make Claude Code slower or less reliable
16	- Local-only operation — zero-cost regime, per-user
17	- Respect Anthropic workspace cache isolation (Feb 5, 2026)
18	
19	**Non-Goals:**
20	- Tokenizer-aware rewriting (Phase 1 M3) — deferred to v1.1
21	- Tracker reconstruction on restart — deferred to v1.1 (one cache-write penalty on cold start is acceptable)
22	- Shared team caches — explicitly out of scope; would leave the zero-cost regime
23	- Retry of Anthropic API 5xx — "that's the API client's job"
24

27	## Infrastructure & Technology Choices
28	
29	| Layer | Choice | Version | Rationale |
30	|-------|--------|---------|-----------|
31	| Language / runtime | TypeScript, Node.js | ≥ 20.10 | Native fetch, stable test runner |
32	| MCP framework | `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` | ^1.x (MIT) | Official MCP; Claude Code ≥ 0.6 required |
33	| API client | `@anthropic-ai/sdk` | ^0.x (MIT) | First-party Anthropic client |
34	| Tokenizer | `@anthropic-ai/tokenizer` | ^0.x | Resolves Opus 4.6 vs 4.7 differences |
35	| Persistent storage | `better-sqlite3` | ^11.x | Synchronous SQLite in WAL mode; prebuilt binaries |
36	| ID generation | `ulid` | ^2.x | Block IDs |
37	| CLI | `commander` | ^12.x | CLI parsing |
38	| Logging | `pino` + `pino-pretty` | ^9.x + ^11.x | Structured logging, daily rotation |
39	| Config validation | `zod` | ^3.x | Schema validation |
40	| Tests | `vitest` + `nock` | ^2.x + ^14.x | Unit/integration; nock for recorded API fixtures |
41	| Build | `tsup` (esbuild) | ^8.x | Dual ESM/CJS output |
42	| Lint | `eslint` + `@typescript-eslint` | ^9.x + ^8.x | Includes `no-restricted-paths` for module layering |
43	| Distribution | npm tarball + Claude Code marketplace | — | GitHub Actions signs provenance |
44	| Hosted services | None (local-only) | — | Optional telemetry endpoint: `https://telemetry.cachelane.dev/v1/report` |

48	## High-Level Architecture
49	
50	### Module Layout (8 modules, strictly layered)
51	
52	```
53	types
54	  ├── config
55	  ├── storage
56	  ├── tokenizer
57	  └── classifier
58	        └── orchestrator   (hot path — classify → prune → reorder per turn)
59	              └── keepalive
60	                    └── server   (MCP server — cachelane:stats, :explain, :expand)
61	                          └── cli
62	```
63	
64	**Dependency rule:** Strict downward-only imports, enforced by `eslint-plugin-import / no-restricted-paths`.
65	Violations **block merge**.
66	
67	### Component Locations
68	
69	| Component | Location |
70	|-----------|----------|
71	| Orchestrator process | RAM |
72	| Classifier, Pruner, Reorderer, Keepalive worker, MCP server, Cache-state tracker | In orchestrator process |
73	| SQLite reference log | `~/.cachelane/cachelane.db` |
74	| Config file | `~/.cachelane/config.json` |
75	| Log files | `~/.cachelane/logs/*.log` (daily rotation, 7-day retention) |
76	| Claude Code integration | `~/.claude/mcp.json` (MCP registration), `~/.claude/hooks/` (hooks) |
77	
78	---
79	
80	## Data Architecture
81	
82	### Storage Tiers
83	
84	| Tier | What's stored | Persistence | Reset condition |
85	|------|--------------|-------------|-----------------|
86	| In-memory (`CacheStateTracker`) | Per-prefix: hash, ttl_class, cached_at_ms, last_read_at_ms, expected_expiry_ms | RAM only | Process restart |
87	| SQLite (`~/.cachelane/cachelane.db`) | Per-block metadata (NOT contents), turn records, reference audit log | On-disk | Explicit purge |
88	| Block content | Transient in orchestrator only | Never persisted | Each turn |
89	
90	**Block contents are NEVER stored anywhere.** Only metadata (id, kind, hashes, counters).
91	
92	### SQLite Schema
93	
94	#### `blocks` table
95	
96	> **Convention:** storage and API-contract types use `snake_case`. In-process working types
97	> may use `camelCase`. Rule of thumb: if it crosses a process/storage/network boundary,
98	> snake_case.
99	
100	```sql
101	CREATE TABLE blocks (
102	  id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
103	  workspace_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
104	  session_id      TEXT NOT NULL,
105	  content_hash    TEXT NOT NULL,
106	  kind            TEXT NOT NULL,
107	  volatility      TEXT NOT NULL,            -- "STABLE" | "SEMI" | "VOLATILE"
108	  is_pinned       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
109	  token_count     INTEGER NOT NULL,
110	  added_at_turn   INTEGER NOT NULL,
111	  last_referenced_at_turn INTEGER NOT NULL,
112	  unused_turns    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
113	  is_stub         INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
114	  stub_summary    TEXT,
115	  refetch_handle  TEXT,
116	  created_at      INTEGER NOT NULL,
117	  updated_at      INTEGER NOT NULL
118	);
119	CREATE INDEX idx_blocks_session ON blocks(workspace_id, session_id);
120	CREATE INDEX idx_blocks_hash    ON blocks(content_hash);
121	CREATE INDEX idx_blocks_unused  ON blocks(unused_turns) WHERE is_stub = 0;
122	```
123	
124	#### `turns` table
125	
126	```sql
127	CREATE TABLE turns (
128	  id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
129	  workspace_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
130	  session_id      TEXT NOT NULL,
131	  turn_number     INTEGER NOT NULL,
132	  model           TEXT NOT NULL,
133	  input_tokens    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
134	  output_tokens   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
135	  cache_creation_5m_tokens   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
136	  cache_creation_1h_tokens   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
137	  cache_read_tokens          INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
138	  effective_cost_units       REAL NOT NULL,
139	  prefix_breakpoint_hash     TEXT,
140	  middle_breakpoint_hash     TEXT,
141	  pruned_blocks_count        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
142	  keepalive_pings_since_last_turn INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
143	  created_at      INTEGER NOT NULL
144	);
145	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_turns_session_num ON turns(workspace_id, session_id, turn_number);
146	```
147	
148	**Effective cost formula (stored at write time):**
149	`effective_cost_units = input_tokens + 1.25 × cache_creation_5m + 2.0 × cache_creation_1h + 0.1 × cache_read`
150	
151	#### `block_references` table (append-only audit log)
152	
153	```sql
154	CREATE TABLE block_references (
155	  id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
156	  block_id        TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES blocks(id),
157	  turn_id         TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES turns(id),
158	  reference_type  TEXT NOT NULL,    -- "tool_call" | "text_quote" | "id_mention"
159	  evidence        TEXT NOT NULL,    -- short string showing what matched
160	  created_at      INTEGER NOT NULL
161	);
162	CREATE INDEX idx_refs_block ON block_references(block_id);
163	CREATE INDEX idx_refs_turn  ON block_references(turn_id);
164	-- 90-day retention cap (enforced at write time once retention job lands; M4+)
165	```
166	
167	### Retention Policy
168	
169	| Data | Retention |
170	|------|-----------|
171	| Log files | 7-day, daily rotation |
172	| `block_references` | 90-day cap |
173	| `blocks` + `turns` | Unbounded in v1 |
174	| Heavy user estimate (5 sessions/day × 50 turns × 1 year) | ~100 MB |
175	
176	### Schema Migration
177	
178	- Migrations in `src/storage/migrations/001_initial.sql`
179	- Config schema versioned (`"version": 1`)
180	- **Newer-than-supported config:** refuse to start
181	- **Older config:** run migration automatically

185	## API Surface
186	
187	### MCP Tools (north-bound, consumed by the model at runtime)
188	
189	| Tool | Inputs | Returns |
190	|------|--------|---------|
191	| `cachelane:stats` | `scope: "session" \| "workspace" \| "all"` (default `session`), `since: ISO duration` | `turns`, `cache_hit_ratio`, `effective_cost_units`, `no_cachelane_cost_units`, `savings_ratio`, pruner stats, keepalive stats |
192	| `cachelane:explain` | `turn: number` (default most recent) | Region breakdown, breakpoint hashes, pruner decisions array with reasons, full usage block |
193	| `cachelane:expand` | `block_id: string` (8-char prefix accepted) | Re-issues original refetch command; restored content enters suffix on next turn |
194	
195	### CLI Commands
196	
197	```
198	cachelane install
199	cachelane stats [--since <ISO-duration>]
200	cachelane stats --opt-in
201	cachelane explain [--turn <N>]
202	cachelane pin <file|glob>
203	cachelane exclude <file|glob>
204	cachelane prune --aggressive | --conservative | --default
205	cachelane keepalive <off|static|adaptive|auto>

211	
212	### Telemetry (opt-in, south-bound)
213	
214	- `POST https://telemetry.cachelane.dev/v1/report` — at most once/hour
215	- Payload: `installation_id`, `version`, `reporting_period_ms`, `turns`, `cache_hit_ratio`,
216	  `pruner_enabled`, `keepalive_policy`, `effective_cost_units_total`, `no_cachelane_baseline_total`
217	- **Forbidden fields:** workspace_id, session_id, file paths, block contents, model name, API key
218	- Response: 204. Default: OFF.

221	
222	## Per-Turn Overhead Budget
223	
224	All orchestrator work must be invisible relative to the Anthropic API round-trip (~200ms RTT).
225	
226	| Operation | Target |
227	|-----------|--------|
228	| Block decomposition | < 1 ms |
229	| Classification (all blocks) | < 2 ms (O(N), N ≤ 200) |
230	| Storage load (counter SELECT) | < 5 ms |
231	| Pruning | < 1 ms (O(N)) |
232	| Reordering + sort | < 2 ms (O(N log N)) |
233	| Breakpoint placement | < 0.5 ms (constant time) |
234	| **Total PreRequest overhead** | **< 12 ms** (< 6% of 200ms RTT) |
235	| Reference detection (PostResponse) | < 10 ms (100 blocks + 10KB response) |
236	| Storage update (counter UPDATE) | < 5 ms |
237	| **Total PostResponse overhead** | **< 20 ms** (response already streaming — latency-invisible) |
238	
239	---
240	
241	## Memory Budget
242	
243	| Component | Budget |
244	|-----------|--------|
245	| Orchestrator process RSS (steady state) | < 50 MB |
246	| Per-session `SessionState` | ~50 KB (100 blocks × ~500 B metadata) |
247	| Cache state tracker | ~1 KB per workspace |
248	| 10 workspaces × 50-turn sessions | ~30 MB |
249	
250	---
251	
252	## Scalability Model
253	
254	- One Claude Code session = one Cachelane MCP server process (~10 MB each)
255	- 20 concurrent sessions → 20 processes
256	- SQLite WAL mode: concurrent readers + serialised writers; < 5 writes/turn is far below SQLite's limits
257	- No central backend, no autoscaling, no shared state across users
258	
259	**Bottleneck:** Signal 3 of reference detection (40-char shingle, O(B × |text|)) is the most
260	expensive operation — bounded at ~5 ms for typical sessions.
261	
262	---
263	
264	## Reliability & Failure Handling
265	
266	**Core principle: fail open.** "Cachelane never makes Claude Code slower or less reliable than it
267	would have been without Cachelane installed."
268	
269	| Failure | Response |
270	|---------|----------|
271	| Anthropic 5xx | Bubble up unchanged; don't retry |
272	| Anthropic 4xx (bad `cache_control`) | Log full payload (key redacted); bubble up; increment counter |
273	| SQLite write failure | Log; drop counter update for this turn; **continue** |
274	| SQLite read failure | Return defaults (`unused_turns = 0`); continue |
275	| SQLite corruption (startup `PRAGMA integrity_check`) | Rename to `.corrupt-{timestamp}`; create empty DB; warn |
276	| Hook exception | Log stack; return unmutated request; pass-through for one turn |
277	| Keepalive ping fails | Log info; retry next interval; do NOT crash worker |
278	| Reference detection panic | Default to "no references"; increment all counters |
279	| Disk full on log rotation | Log to stderr; skip persistence; continue |
280	| Malformed config JSON | Fall back to defaults; refuse to overwrite user config |
281	| Config schema newer than supported | Refuse to start |
282	| Config schema older | Run migration |
283	| Process restart | Cache state tracker resets; next turn pays one cache-write penalty |
284	
285	**Graceful shutdown:** SIGTERM → stop accepting hooks → finish in-flight → flush storage → exit
286	within 2 seconds.
287	
288	**Self-diagnosis:** `cachelane doctor` checks Node ≥ 20.10, Claude Code ≥ 0.6, hook registration,
289	MCP registration, SQLite writability, config parseability, recent cache-hit ratio (warns if < 0.2
290	sustained over 50 turns — indicates prefix-instability bug).
291	
292	---
293	
294	## Security & Privacy
295	
296	**Trust boundaries:**
297	- User's local machine = trusted
298	- Claude Code = trusted (host process)
299	- Anthropic API = trusted at TLS protocol level
300	- Block content = untrusted for logs/telemetry (may contain secrets)
301	
302	**API key handling:** Read from environment; never written to disk, logs, or memory beyond request
303	lifetime. Telemetry payload allowlist prevents key inclusion.
304	
305	**Log sanitisation:**
306	- `info` (default): never includes block content
307	- `debug`: may include `block_id`, `kind`, `token_count`, `content_hash` — never raw content
308	- `trace` (per-session opt-in): first 200 chars of content with regex redaction for secret patterns
309	  (`sk-...`, Bearer tokens, AWS keys)
310	
311	**MCP attack surface:**
312	- `cachelane:stats` / `cachelane:explain` — aggregate numbers/metadata only; cannot leak content
313	- `cachelane:expand` — `block_id` looked up in local SQLite; refetch command restored from DB,
314	  NOT constructed from input → prevents injection
315	
316	**Supply chain:**
317	- npm provenance (signed attestation linking tarball to source commit via GitHub Actions)
318	- All deps pinned exactly in `package-lock.json`
319	- Dependabot PRs reviewed by two engineers
320	- Weekly `npm audit` + GitHub Advanced Security; fails build on high-severity unpatched vulns
321

324	## Observability
325	
326	| Signal | Implementation |
327	|--------|---------------|
328	| Logging | Pino structured JSON, `~/.cachelane/logs/*.log`, daily rotation, 7-day retention |
329	| Metrics | `cachelane:stats` and `cachelane stats` CLI — cache_hit_ratio, effective_cost_units, savings_ratio, pruner/keepalive stats |
330	| Per-turn trace | `cachelane:explain` — region breakdown, breakpoint placement, pruner decisions, full usage block |
331	| Alerting | `cachelane doctor` warns if cache_hit_ratio < 0.2 for > 50 consecutive turns |
332	| Post-release monitoring | Expected band 0.5–0.85; persistent dip below 0.3 = regression |

335	
336	## Deployment & Operations
337	
338	**Environments:** Local only. No staging/prod fleet. Dev/test via vitest + nock fixtures.
339	
340	**CI gates (every PR must pass all):**
341	1. vitest + lint + cache-stability test + corpus gate (where applicable)
342	2. Spec-to-code diff attached; vocabulary drift reconciled (must use `STABLE | SEMI | VOLATILE`)
343	3. No new mutable global state, non-deterministic hot-path ops, `Date.now`/`Math.random`/Map
344	   iteration order in prefix path
345	4. No new dependencies without explicit reviewer approval
346	5. PR description explains why
347	6. Primary + secondary reviewer approval
348	7. If touching `cache_control` placement: cache-stability test passes **twice on independent runs**
349	
350	**Review model:**
351	- Primary reviewer: deep review (30 min routine, up to 90 min for cache-stability/pruner)
352	- Secondary reviewer: 10-min sanity pass for scope creep and vocabulary drift
353	- High-stakes PRs (cache_control placement, K-pruner, API client): both engineers deep-review
354

357	## Milestones
358	
359	| Milestone | Deliverables | Gate |
360	|-----------|-------------|------|
361	| **M1: Foundations** | `types`, `config`, `storage`, `tokenizer` modules; SQLite schema applied; tokenizer model-lookup test passes for 4.6 and 4.7 | Unit tests per module |
362	| **M2: Classifier** | `classifier` module; classifies every `BlockKind` correctly against fixture set | Unit tests covering all §3.2 BlockKind entries |
363	| **M3: Orchestrator core** | Reorderer, breakpoint placement, request mutation. No pruning yet. | **Cache-stability test active and gating**: SHA-256 of prefix region byte-identical across 3 consecutive identical-input runs |
364	| **M4: Reference detection** | PostResponse hook, reference detection, storage updates. **PREREQUISITE: 100-session annotated corpus complete before M4 begins (6-hour human annotation task; start during M2 or M3 so it's ready in time)** | Precision ≥ 95%, recall ≥ 85% on corpus (CI gate) |
365	| **M5: K-pruner** | Stub materialisation, refetch flow, `cachelane:expand` | Integration test: 6-turn synthetic session; pruning kicks in at turn 4 |
366	| **M6: Keepalive** | Background worker, adaptive + auto policies | Time-mocked integration test: verify pings fire only when expected |
367	| **M7: MCP server + CLI** | `cachelane:stats`, `cachelane:explain` wired; all CLI commands | E2E test against recorded Claude Code session |
368	| **M8: Polish + benchmark** | Docs, README, benchmark harness, optional keepalive experiment (§2.4) | Empirical benchmark report; `doctor` passes on macOS, Linux, Windows |
369	| **M9: Release** | npm publish with provenance; Claude Code marketplace listing | Tag, release notes, manual smoke test on fresh machine |
370	
371	**Critical dependency:** The 100-session annotated corpus gates M4 and must start during M2 or M3.

400	## Operational Runbook Hooks
401	
402	| Condition | Action |
403	|-----------|--------|
404	| Installation issue | `cachelane doctor` — checks Node version, Claude Code version, hooks, MCP registration, SQLite writability |
405	| Suspected Cachelane bug / A-B test | `cachelane disable` → hooks become no-ops → `cachelane enable` to restore |
406	| Reinstall | `cachelane install --force` (idempotent; refuses to overwrite without `--force`) |
407	| Full wipe | `cachelane uninstall --purge` (removes `~/.cachelane/` data) |
408	| SQLite corruption | Automatic: rename to `.corrupt-{timestamp}`, create empty DB, warn in log |
409	| Hook exception | Automatic: log stack, return unmutated request, pass-through for one turn |
410	| Pruner too aggressive | `cachelane prune --conservative` (K=5) |
411	| Keepalive tuning | `cachelane keepalive <off|static|adaptive|auto>` |
412	| `/compact` detected | Automatic: reset `unused_turns` for replaced blocks, classify compacted summary as `SEMI`, defer middle breakpoint until seen twice |

1	# 03 — Engineering Specifications
2	
3	**Purpose:** All functional/non-functional requirements, API contracts, data models, acceptance criteria.  
4	**Scope:** Phase 2 implementation requirements — what must be built and how it will be verified.  
5	**Source:** `Cachelane_Phase2_Engineering_Specifications_v2_1.docx` (v2, May 2026).
6	
7	> **Section-numbering note:** The original doc had a printing bug where §6 "Agent implementation
8	> guardrails" printed its subsections as §7.1–7.7. Fixed in v2 — all references below use the
9	> corrected §6.x numbering.

11	---
12	
13	## Functional Requirements
14	
15	### REQ-F-001 through REQ-F-010 — Transport & Storage Foundation
16	
17	| ID | Requirement | Source |
18	|----|-------------|--------|
19	| REQ-F-001 | The system SHALL be distributed as an MCP server using `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (TypeScript) over **stdio transport** (no network ports). | §1.1, D8 |
20	| REQ-F-002 | The orchestrator SHALL read Anthropic API usage fields on every turn: `cache_creation_input_tokens`, `cache_read_input_tokens`, `ephemeral_5m_input_tokens`, `ephemeral_1h_input_tokens` via `@anthropic-ai/sdk`. | §1.1 |
21	| REQ-F-003 | Block-size accounting SHALL use `@anthropic-ai/tokenizer` with a **model-string lookup** to select the correct tokenizer per request (supports both Opus 4.6 and 4.7). | §1.1, Q8 |
22	| REQ-F-004 | Persistent storage SHALL be a single-file SQLite database via `better-sqlite3` in WAL mode. No external store. | §1.1, §1.2.2, D5 |
23	| REQ-F-005 | In-memory cache-state tracking SHALL live in the orchestrator process using plain `Map`/`Set`; restart recovery relies on the on-disk SQLite log as the authoritative source. | §1.1 |
24	| REQ-F-006 | Hashing for prefix fingerprinting and block deduplication SHALL use SHA-256 via `node:crypto`. | §1.1 |
25	| REQ-F-007 | The CLI SHALL be implemented with `commander`. | §1.1 |
26	| REQ-F-008 | Logging SHALL be structured JSON via `pino`, with `pino-pretty` in dev. Log lines SHALL include billable usage fields. | §1.1, §1.4 |
27	| REQ-F-009 | Local logs SHALL be written to `~/.cachelane/logs/`, **rotated daily**, with **7-day retention**. | §1.4 |
28	| REQ-F-010 | The system SHALL expose a `cachelane stats` CLI command that reads directly from the SQLite log (no external metrics service). | §1.4 |
29	
30	### REQ-F-011 through REQ-F-020 — Telemetry, Privacy & Security
31	
32	| ID | Requirement | Source |
33	|----|-------------|--------|
34	| REQ-F-011 | The system SHALL implement opt-in anonymous telemetry reporting cache-hit ratio, average reduction, and model used; **default OFF**; permanently disable-able. | §1.4 |
35	| REQ-F-012 | Telemetry MUST NOT be enabled by default; activation requires an explicit command. | §1.4, Q7, D9 |
36	| REQ-F-013 | All processing SHALL occur locally. No request bodies, prompts, or completions may leave the user's machine except in the direct path to `api.anthropic.com`. | §1.5, D7 |
37	| REQ-F-014 | The reference log SHALL store **block IDs, classifications, token counts, usage counters only** — NOT block contents. | §1.5 |
38	| REQ-F-015 | Block contents SHALL live only transiently in the orchestrator process and SHALL NOT be persisted. | §1.5 |
39	| REQ-F-016 | The Anthropic API key SHALL be read from the user's existing Claude Code config; Cachelane MUST NOT read, store, or forward it outside the originating API call. | §1.5 |
40	| REQ-F-017 | The system SHALL respect Anthropic workspace-level cache isolation (effective since Feb 2026): Cachelane MUST use Claude Code's existing workspace ID and MUST NOT share prefixes across workspaces. | §1.5, Q6 |
41	| REQ-F-018 | The reference log SHALL be keyed by workspace ID. | Q6 |
42	| REQ-F-019 | The system SHALL implement a synthetic **keepalive ping** mechanism: a minimal API call (`max_tokens=1`, one-token user message) that touches the same prefix to reset its TTL. | §2.1 |
43	| REQ-F-020 | Keepalive pings MUST NOT fire when a real turn just touched the cache (idle-only triggering). | §2.2, B1 |
44	
45	### REQ-F-021 through REQ-F-030 — Orchestration Logic
46	
47	| ID | Requirement | Source |
48	|----|-------------|--------|
49	| REQ-F-021 | The default ship configuration SHALL be a **hybrid keepalive policy**: adaptive 4-minute idle trigger for short prefixes, automatically switching to 1-hour TTL when prefix size P > 50k tokens. | §2.4.6 |
50	| REQ-F-022 | A `cachelane.config.json` (`~/.cachelane/config.json`) SHALL expose manual override knobs for keepalive policy and all other configurable parameters. | §2.4.6 |
51	| REQ-F-023 | The K-pruner SHALL use a **three-signal deterministic reference detector**: (a) file paths quoted in tool calls, (b) block IDs cited in assistant text, (c) 40-character shingle exact substring overlap. | §1.2.3, Q1 |
52	| REQ-F-024 | Cachelane SHALL inject a short ID prefix on every stubbable block (used by the reference detector and for refetch via `cachelane:expand`). | §1.2.3 |
53	| REQ-F-025 | The system MUST NOT use embeddings, vector stores, or any ML model dependency for reference detection. | §1.2.3, D6 |
54	| REQ-F-026 | The K-pruner default value of K SHALL be **K=3**, with `--aggressive` mode (K=2) and `--conservative` mode (K=5). | Q3 |
55	| REQ-F-027 | The classifier SHALL be fingerprint-based and **conservative**: a block defaults to `VOLATILE` unless it matches a stable signature (hash of file paths + last-modified mtimes for stable candidates). | Q2 |
56	| REQ-F-028 | The reorderer MUST NOT reorder `tool_use`/`tool_result` pairs individually; it MAY only move whole pairs as a unit (invariant preserves byte-identical prefix). | Q9 |
57	| REQ-F-029 | A second `cache_control` breakpoint at the middle-suffix boundary SHALL be placed **only if** the same turn-window has been seen byte-identical at least twice (dynamic placement). | Q4 |
58	| REQ-F-030 | When Claude Code's `/compact` runs, Cachelane SHALL treat the new compacted history as a fresh middle region and reset all per-block counters for replaced blocks. | Q5 |
59	
60	### REQ-F-031 through REQ-F-037 — Distribution & Packaging
61	
62	| ID | Requirement | Source |
63	|----|-------------|--------|
64	| REQ-F-031 | The interaction with `/compact` SHALL be documented in the README. | Q5 |
65	| REQ-F-032 | The system SHALL be packaged as an npm package and dual-published as a Claude Code plugin via `.claude-plugin` manifest. | §1.1 |
66	| REQ-F-033 | Releases SHALL be signed via **npm provenance** through GitHub Actions. | §1.1 |
67	| REQ-F-034 | Build/bundling SHALL be performed with `tsup` (esbuild), emitting both ESM and CJS. | §1.1 |
68	| REQ-F-035 | The CLI SHALL provide a privacy doc in the README and a `cachelane stats --opt-in` command for telemetry opt-in. | Q7, D9 |
69	| REQ-F-036 | `[INFERRED]` Stubs created by the K-pruner SHALL preserve the block's identifier and be refetchable on demand — pruning is non-lossy at the application layer. | Project context |
70	| REQ-F-037 | `[INFERRED]` The orchestrator SHALL forward requests directly to `api.anthropic.com` on the user's behalf (no gateway). | §3.3 |

94	| REQ-NF-010 | Cache-stability assertion | Request bodies byte-identical (SHA-256) from prompt start through `cache_control` breakpoint across 3 consecutive identical-input runs | §6.2 |
95	| REQ-NF-011 | Hot-path performance characterisation | Orchestrator work is microseconds (1 SHA-256 + 1 classification + 1 SQLite write); network is the bottleneck | §1.2.1 |

103	
104	| ID | Requirement | Target | Source |
105	|----|-------------|--------|--------|
106	| REQ-NF-017 | Total agent token spend target | **$180–$340** to v1.0 | §3.4.1 |
107	| REQ-NF-018 | Upfront cash spend | **$0** (or $20 optional domain; $80 if separate API account) | §3.2 |
108	| REQ-NF-019 | Recurring cash spend | **$0** (up to $10/mo only if telemetry enabled and > 100k users) | §3.3 |

110	| REQ-NF-021 | Cache-stability test (breakpoint-change PRs) | Must pass **twice on independent runs** | §6.5 |

121	
122	## API Contracts
123	
124	### C-1: Anthropic Messages API (south-bound, consumed)
125	
126	- **Client SDK:** `@anthropic-ai/sdk`
127	- **Required response fields read per turn:**
128	  - `cache_creation_input_tokens`
129	  - `cache_read_input_tokens`
130	  - `ephemeral_5m_input_tokens`
131	  - `ephemeral_1h_input_tokens`
132	- **Cache semantics:** byte-exact prefix matching with TTL. Workspace-isolated since Feb 2026.
133	- **Pricing:** 0.1× read, 1.25× 5-min write, 2.0× 1-hour write.
134	
135	### C-2: Keepalive Ping Request
136	
137	- **Payload:** one-token user message; `max_tokens=1`; identical prefix to current session
138	- **Effect:** resets the prefix's TTL
139	- **Per-ping billable cost (model):** ~`0.1 × P + 5` base-input-token units
140	
141	### C-3: MCP Server (Cachelane ↔ Claude Code)
142	
143	- **Transport:** stdio (no network port)
144	- **SDK:** `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` ^1.x
145	- **Host requirement:** Claude Code ≥ 0.6
146	- **MCP tools exposed:** `cachelane:stats`, `cachelane:explain`, `cachelane:expand`
147	
148	### C-4: Telemetry Endpoint (opt-in, operated by project)
149	
150	- **URL:** `https://telemetry.cachelane.dev/v1/report`
151	- **Default state:** OFF
152	- **Payload fields (allowed):** `installation_id` (random UUID), `version`, `reporting_period_ms`, `turns`, `cache_hit_ratio`, `pruner_enabled`, `keepalive_policy`, `effective_cost_units_total`, `no_cachelane_baseline_total`
153	- **Payload fields (forbidden):** `workspace_id`, `session_id`, file paths, block contents, model name, API key, IP-correlatable timestamps
154	- **Rate:** at most once per hour per installation
155	- **Response:** 204
156	
157	### C-5: CLI Surface (commander)
158	
159	```
160	cachelane install
161	cachelane stats [--since <ISO-duration>]
162	cachelane stats --opt-in
163	cachelane explain [--turn <N>]
164	cachelane pin <file|glob>
165	cachelane exclude <file|glob>
166	cachelane prune --aggressive | --conservative | --default
167	cachelane keepalive <off|static|adaptive|auto>
168	cachelane disable
169	cachelane enable
170	cachelane doctor
171	cachelane uninstall [--purge]
172	```
173	
174	### C-6: Configuration File
175	
176	- **Path:** `~/.cachelane/config.json`
177	- **Schema version field:** `"version": 1`
178	- **Key fields (with defaults):**
179	
180	| Field | Default | Range/values | Effect |
181	|-------|---------|-------------|--------|
182	| `pruner.enabled` | `true` | boolean | Toggle K-pruning |
183	| `pruner.k` | `3` | 1–10 | Idle-turn threshold |
184	| `pruner.mode` | `"default"` | `default \| conservative \| aggressive` | Overrides K |
185	| `keepalive.policy` | `"auto"` | `off \| static \| adaptive \| auto` | Keepalive strategy |
186	| `keepalive.interval_seconds` | `150` | positive int | Check loop interval |
187	| `keepalive.idle_threshold_seconds` | `240` | positive int | Min idle before ping |
188	| `keepalive.large_prefix_threshold_tokens` | `50000` | positive int | Switch to 1h TTL above this |
189	| `classification.sliding_window_turns` | `4` | positive int | Recent-turn window size |
190	| `telemetry.opt_in` | `false` | boolean | Telemetry opt-in |
191	| `log_level` | `"info"` | `trace \| debug \| info \| warn \| error` | Pino log level |
192	
193	- **Migration:** newer-than-supported config schema → refuse to start; older → run migration
194	
195	---
196	
197	## Data Models
198	
199	> **Convention:** storage and API-contract types use `snake_case` (e.g. the `Block` interface
200	> below, the `blocks/turns/block_references` rows, and `CachelaneConfig` fields). In-process
201	> working types (function parameters, request payloads handled in code) may use `camelCase`.
202	> Rule of thumb: if it crosses a process / storage / network boundary, snake_case.
203	
204	### Entity: Block (in-memory, transient)
205	
206	```typescript
207	interface Block {
208	  // Identity
209	  id: string;                       // ULID; assigned on first observation
210	  workspace_id: string;             // Claude Code workspace identifier
211	  session_id: string;               // Claude Code session identifier
212	
213	  // Classification
214	  kind: BlockKind;                  // see enum below — 11 values
215	  volatility: Volatility;           // "STABLE" | "SEMI" | "VOLATILE"
216	  is_pinned: boolean;               // true if matched by config.classification.pin
217	
218	  // Content (transient; never persisted)
219	  // content: AnthropicContentBlock — deferred to M2 (no consumer in M1)
220	  content_hash: string;             // SHA-256 of canonical serialization
221	
222	  // Accounting
223	  token_count: number;              // tokens under the current model's tokenizer
224	  added_at_turn: number;            // turn number when first observed
225	  last_referenced_at_turn: number;  // turn number of most recent reference
226	  unused_turns: number;             // counter for K-pruning; starts at 0
227	
228	  // Stub state
229	  is_stub: boolean;                 // true if currently materialized as a stub
230	  stub_summary: string | null;      // one-line summary if stubbed; else null
231	  refetch_handle: string | null;    // tool invocation to restore content
232	}
233	
234	type BlockKind =
235	  | "system_prompt"
236	  | "tool_schema"
237	  | "claude_md"
238	  | "project_rules"
239	  | "prior_turn"
240	  | "tool_use_result_pair"
241	  | "file_read"
242	  | "retrieval_result"
243	  | "tool_output"
244	  | "user_message"
245	  | "stub";
246	```
247	
248	### Entity: ReferenceLog row (SQLite `blocks` table)
249	
250	```sql
251	CREATE TABLE blocks (
252	  id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,         -- ULID
253	  workspace_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
254	  session_id      TEXT NOT NULL,
255	  content_hash    TEXT NOT NULL,
256	  kind            TEXT NOT NULL,
257	  volatility      TEXT NOT NULL,            -- "STABLE" | "SEMI" | "VOLATILE"
258	  is_pinned       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
259	  token_count     INTEGER NOT NULL,
260	  added_at_turn   INTEGER NOT NULL,
261	  last_referenced_at_turn INTEGER NOT NULL,
262	  unused_turns    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
263	  is_stub         INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
264	  stub_summary    TEXT,
265	  refetch_handle  TEXT,
266	  created_at      INTEGER NOT NULL,
267	  updated_at      INTEGER NOT NULL
268	);
269	CREATE INDEX idx_blocks_session ON blocks(workspace_id, session_id);
270	CREATE INDEX idx_blocks_hash    ON blocks(content_hash);
271	CREATE INDEX idx_blocks_unused  ON blocks(unused_turns) WHERE is_stub = 0;
272	```
273	
274	**Block contents are NEVER persisted** — only metadata.
275	
276	### Entity: Turn (SQLite `turns` table)
277	
278	```sql
279	CREATE TABLE turns (
280	  id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
281	  workspace_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
282	  session_id      TEXT NOT NULL,
283	  turn_number     INTEGER NOT NULL,
284	  model           TEXT NOT NULL,
285	  input_tokens    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
286	  output_tokens   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
287	  cache_creation_5m_tokens   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
288	  cache_creation_1h_tokens   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
289	  cache_read_tokens          INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
290	  effective_cost_units       REAL NOT NULL,  -- computed at write time
291	  prefix_breakpoint_hash     TEXT,
292	  middle_breakpoint_hash     TEXT,
293	  pruned_blocks_count        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
294	  keepalive_pings_since_last_turn INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
295	  created_at      INTEGER NOT NULL
296	);
297	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_turns_session_num ON turns(workspace_id, session_id, turn_number);
298	```
299	
300	Column names (`cache_creation_5m_tokens`, `cache_creation_1h_tokens`) deliberately mirror the
301	Anthropic API response fields (`ephemeral_5m_input_tokens`, `ephemeral_1h_input_tokens`,
302	`cache_creation_input_tokens`) so the orchestrator does not need a translation layer.
303	
304	### Entity: BlockReference (SQLite `block_references` table, append-only audit log)
305	
306	```sql
307	CREATE TABLE block_references (
308	  id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
309	  block_id        TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES blocks(id),
310	  turn_id         TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES turns(id),
311	  reference_type  TEXT NOT NULL,    -- "tool_call" | "text_quote" | "id_mention"
312	  evidence        TEXT NOT NULL,    -- short string showing what matched
313	  created_at      INTEGER NOT NULL
314	);
315	CREATE INDEX idx_refs_block ON block_references(block_id);
316	CREATE INDEX idx_refs_turn  ON block_references(turn_id);
317	-- 90-day retention cap (enforced at write time once retention job lands; M4+)
318	```
319	
320	### Entity: CacheStateTracker (in-memory only)
321	
322	```typescript
323	interface PrefixState {
324	  workspace_id: string;
325	  prefix_hash: string;
326	  middle_hash: string | null;
327	  prefix_token_count: number;
328	  ttl_class: "5m" | "1h";
329	  cached_at_ms: number;
330	  last_read_at_ms: number;
331	  expected_expiry_ms: number;
332	}
333	// Map<workspace_id, PrefixState>
334	// Resets on process restart; next turn pays one cache-write penalty
335	```
336	
337	### Entity: Turn (logical model)
338	
339	- One user message + one assistant reply (tool calls inside the reply do not split the turn)
340	- Contains zero or more `(tool_use, tool_result)` pairs
341	- The Reorderer moves `(tool_use, tool_result)` pairs **only as atomic units** (splitting would
342	  invalidate the prefix from that point on)
343

346	## Acceptance Criteria
347	
348	| ID | Criterion | REQ mapping |
349	|----|-----------|-------------|
350	| AC-1 | Cache-stability test fires the same logical prompt 3× against a recorded fixture; request bodies byte-identical (SHA-256) from prompt start through `cache_control` breakpoint | REQ-NF-010, REQ-NF-020 |
351	| AC-2 | Cache-stability suite includes ≥ 5 scenarios (empty schemas; large schemas; middle included; middle empty; active-pruning stub-just-created) | REQ-NF-012 |
352	| AC-3 | Cache-stability test failure blocks merge with no exceptions | REQ-NF-020 |
353	| AC-4 | PRs modifying `cache_control` breakpoint placement pass cache-stability test twice on independent runs | REQ-NF-021 |
354	| AC-5 | Reference-detection precision ≥ 95% and recall ≥ 85% against the 100-session corpus, asserted in CI | REQ-NF-008, REQ-NF-009 |
355	| AC-6 | The 100-session annotated corpus exists in-repo **before** any pruner code is written | §6.3 Step 1 |
356	| AC-7 | Each major component PR includes a spec-to-code diff document mapping every named spec concept to its code symbol | §6.4 |
357	| AC-8 | _removed_ — see REQ-NF-013 waiver. M1 scaffolding bundle grandfathered; no PR LOC cap enforced. | _n/a_ |
358	| AC-9 | Every PR includes its own tests; no "tests in follow-up" merges | REQ-NF-014 |
359	| AC-10 | Reviewer checklist applied to every PR: CI green; spec-to-code diff reviewed; no new non-deterministic ops/timestamps/Date.now/Math.random/Map iteration in hot paths; new deps audited; PR description explains "why" | §6.6 |
360	| AC-11 | Keepalive experiment: 11 conditions × 5 scenarios × 30 sessions; report mean/median/95% CI of net effective cost; paired Wilcoxon signed-rank vs A1 baseline | §2.4.5 |
361	| AC-12 | Effective input-token cost computed as `input_tokens + 1.25×cache_creation_5m + 2.0×cache_creation_1h + 0.1×cache_read` | REQ-NF-022 |
362	| AC-13 | v1 keepalive default shipped as the empirically-derived hybrid policy after §2 experiments | REQ-F-021 |
363	| AC-14 | Tokenizer selected via model-string table lookup; validated for both Opus 4.6 and 4.7 | REQ-F-003, Q8 |
364	| AC-15 | Reference log keyed by workspace ID; verified locally that no prefix is shared across workspaces | REQ-F-017, REQ-F-018 |
365	| AC-16 | Reorderer test asserts `(tool_use, tool_result)` pairs are only moved together | REQ-F-028 |
366	| AC-17 | Local logs rotated daily with 7-day retention in `~/.cachelane/logs/` | REQ-F-009 |
367	| AC-18 | Telemetry: off by default; opt-in via explicit command; documented privacy policy in README | REQ-F-011, REQ-F-012 |
368	| AC-19 | Steady-state memory < 50 MB; installed disk < 5 MB; reference log ~1 KB/turn | REQ-NF-004 to REQ-NF-006 |
369	| AC-20 | npm package emits dual ESM/CJS via tsup; publishes with npm provenance from GitHub Actions on tag | REQ-F-032 to REQ-F-034 |
370	| AC-21 | Phase 2 readiness checklist (all 7 rows) approved before systems-design authoring begins | §7 |
371	
372	---
373	
374	## Edge Cases & Failure Modes
375	
376	| # | Scenario | Required behaviour |
377	|---|----------|--------------------|
378	| E1 | Process restart | SQLite reference log is authoritative; in-memory cache state resets; next turn pays one cache-write penalty |
379	| E2 | `/compact` runs | Reset `unused_turns` for all replaced blocks; classify compacted summary as `SEMI`; defer middle breakpoint until seen byte-identical twice |
380	| E3 | `(tool_use, tool_result)` pair split by reorderer | **Must not happen.** Pairs are only moved as atomic units (REQ-F-028). A split invalidates the prefix from that point. |
381	| E4 | Tokenizer drift Opus 4.6 → 4.7 | Up to 35% more tokens for same text; must use model-string lookup (REQ-F-003) or cost predictions are wrong |
382	| E5 | Cache TTL expiry between turns (idle session) | Keepalive worker mitigates; TTL expiry is also tracked as an eviction-event metric |
383	| E6 | Multi-workspace prefix sharing | Violates Anthropic's Feb 2026 isolation; must key reference log by workspace ID (REQ-F-017, REQ-F-018) |
384	| E7 | Middle-region speculative cache write (unstable middle) | Wastes 1.25× write; mitigated by the "twice seen" heuristic (REQ-F-029) |
385	| E8 | SQLite write failure | Log; drop the per-block counter update for this turn; **continue** (fail-open) |
386	| E9 | SQLite corruption (detected on startup) | Rename to `cachelane.db.corrupt-{timestamp}`; create new empty DB; warn user |
387	| E10 | Hook exception | Log stack; return unmutated request; pass-through for one turn (fail-open) |
388	| E11 | Non-deterministic serialisation / timestamp leakage in prefix | Cache-stability test catches this; `Date.now`, `Math.random`, `Map` iteration order are flagged in PR checklist |
389	| E12 | Refetch unavailable (block's original source gone) | `[INFERRED]` Must handle gracefully; return error to model, do not crash |
390	| E13 | Two Claude Code instances sharing same SQLite | WAL mode handles concurrent readers + serialised writers safely |
391	| E14 | `better-sqlite3` native build failure | Prebuilt binaries used; `cachelane doctor` checks Node version |
392	| E15 | Config schema newer than supported | Refuse to start (avoid silent demotion) |
393	| E16 | Config schema older than current | Run migration automatically |
394	
395	---
396	
397	## Phase 2 Scope Boundaries
398	
399	**In scope (v1.0):**
400	- M1 — cache-aware orchestration (classify → prune → reorder + breakpoints + keepalive)
401	- M2 — trajectory-aware K-pruning
402	- MCP server over stdio
403	- SQLite reference log
404	- CLI (install, stats, explain, pin, exclude, prune, keepalive, disable/enable, doctor, uninstall)
405	- Opt-in telemetry (off by default)
406	- Dual ESM/CJS npm package + Claude Code plugin marketplace listing
407	
408	**Deferred to v1.1+:**
409	- M3 — tokenizer-aware rewriting (D3, fast-follow)
410	- M4 — speculative inclusion (overlaps Token Savior)
411	- M5 — cross-session decision distillation (overlaps memsearch)
412	- Tracker reconstruction on restart
413	- Shared team caches
414	
415	**Explicitly rejected:**
416	- Embeddings / vector stores / ML model dependencies (D6)
417	- External Redis / DuckDB
418	- JSON-on-disk storage
419	- Hosted backend / cloud infrastructure (D7)
420	- External APM (Sentry, Datadog)
421	- Formal verification / model checkers
422	
423	---
424	
425	## Open Questions
426	
427	| ID | Question | Owner | Target |
428	|----|----------|-------|--------|
429	| Q001 | Reference-detection precision and recall on real sessions | Lead engineer | 2 weeks after Phase 2 approval |
430	| Q002 | Classifier accuracy on real-world content | Lead engineer | 2 weeks (parallel with Q001) |
431	| Q003 | Optimal K value(s) — K=3 is a starting point | Lead engineer | 3 weeks after approval |
432	| Q007 | Telemetry: off-by-default confirmed; `--opt-in` command shape TBD | Project lead | Before v1 release |
433	| Q008 | Tokenizer model-string table for Opus 4.6 and 4.7 | Lead engineer | 2 days |
434	
435	See [`07-open-questions.md`](07-open-questions.md) for complete list.
436

1	# 01 — System Overview
2	
3	**Purpose:** High-level product summary, goals, non-goals, and full glossary.  
4	**Scope:** Product-level understanding; no implementation details.  
5	**Sources:** All five source documents.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## What Cachelane Is
10	
11	Cachelane is a **local MCP server + Claude Code hooks** (PreRequest/PostResponse) that intercepts
12	every conversation turn between Claude Code and `api.anthropic.com`. It does two things:
13	
14	1. **Cache-aware prompt orchestration** — classifies each content block by volatility and reorders
15	   the prompt into three regions (stable prefix, semi-stable middle, volatile suffix), placing two
16	   `cache_control` breakpoints so Anthropic's prompt cache fires at **0.1×** the normal input cost
17	   on cache hits instead of paying **1.0×** every turn.
18	
19	2. **K-pruning** — after each turn, any tool-call result block that has been idle for ≥ K
20	   consecutive turns is replaced with a compact stub that preserves its identifier and is
21	   refetchable on demand. This flattens token growth in long sessions without discarding any
22	   information.
23	
24	Cachelane is **a complement to, not a replacement for**, existing context tools (Context Mode,
25	Code Graph, Token Savior, etc.). It operates on the final assembled prompt, after every other tool
26	has shaped its portion of context.
27	
28	**Positioning tag:** "A cache-discipline layer for Claude Code."
29	
30	---
31	
32	## Goals
33	
34	| # | Goal |
35	|---|------|
36	| G1 | Reduce input-token costs on every subsequent turn via K-pruning |
37	| G2 | Maximize Anthropic prompt-cache hit rate by reordering blocks into stable/semi/volatile regions with two `cache_control` breakpoints |
38	| G3 | Refresh prompt-cache TTL via a keepalive worker so users don't pay full re-write penalties after idle periods |
39	| G4 | Fail open: never make Claude Code slower or less reliable than without Cachelane |
40	| G5 | Provide local-only operation — zero-cost regime, per-user, no shared backend |
41	| G6 | Respect Anthropic workspace cache isolation (effective Feb 5, 2026) |
42	
43	---
44	
45	## Non-Goals
46	
47	| # | Non-Goal | Disposition |
48	|---|----------|-------------|
49	| NG1 | Tokenizer-aware content rewriting | Deferred to v1.1 (Phase 1 M3) |
50	| NG2 | Speculative / budgeted block inclusion | Deferred (overlaps Token Savior) |
51	| NG3 | Cross-session decision distillation | Rejected (overlaps memsearch) |
52	| NG4 | Shared team caches / multi-user fleet | Out of scope; would leave the zero-cost regime |
53	| NG5 | Hosted backend or cloud infrastructure | Explicitly rejected (see [ADR-007](decisions/ADR-007-local-only.md)) |
54	| NG6 | Retry of Anthropic API 5xx errors | That is the API client's responsibility |
55	| NG7 | Tracker reconstruction on process restart | Deferred to v1.1 (one cache-write penalty on cold start is acceptable) |
56	
57	---
58	
59	## Product Positioning
60	
61	**The gap Cachelane fills.** Seven major Claude Code token-reduction tools converged on four
62	reduction axes: upstream filtering, structural pre-indexing, progressive disclosure, and
63	output-side compression. None of them addresses the **prompt cache** — the largest single
64	unexploited cost lever in the Claude API.
65	
66	Reported real-world cache-hit ratios *after deliberate prefix design* are **50–84%**
67	(Helicone, Vellum, ProjectDiscovery). Rates below 20% are classified as a prefix-design failure,
68	not a model limitation. The naively-routed prompt pays 1.0× input cost every turn instead of 0.1×
69	on cache reads — a missed ~10× discount on the largest cost surface.
70	
71	**Expected impact (v1):**
72	- Cache-aware orchestration (M1): **30–60% savings** on cacheable workloads
73	- K-pruning (M2): additional **+10–15 percentage points** on 15-turn sessions at K=3
74	- Combined target: ≥ 40% effective input-cost reduction vs. the B1 starter stack baseline
75	
76	---
77	
78	## Anthropic Prompt Cache Mechanics
79	
80	| Operation | Price multiplier | Notes |
81	|-----------|-----------------|-------|
82	| Cache read | **0.1×** base input | 90% discount |
83	| 5-minute cache write | **1.25×** base input | Default TTL |
84	| 1-hour cache write | **2.0×** base input | Break-even at 2 reads |
85	| Cache match requirement | byte-identical prefix to `cache_control` breakpoint | Any change invalidates from that point |
86	| Workspace isolation | per-workspace | Enforced by Anthropic since Feb 5, 2026 |
87	
88	**Worked example:** 30k-token prefix reused across 10 turns.
89	- Naïve (no cache): 1.0× × 10 = **10.0 prefix-units**
90	- Cache-aware: 1.25× (one write) + 0.1× × 9 (nine reads) = **2.15 prefix-units** → **4.6× reduction**
91	
92	---
93	
94	## Glossary
95	
96	| Term | Definition |
97	|------|------------|
98	| **Turn** | One user message plus the assistant's full reply (tool calls inside the reply do not split the turn). |
99	| **Tool call** | An assistant-initiated function invocation. Multiple tool calls per turn are allowed; they do not advance the turn counter. |
100	| **Content block** | A single tool-call result; the atomic unit that K-pruning operates on. |
